title: Enhancing survival risk prediction through imputation and feature selection in high-dimensional protein biomarker data
task: Survival risk prediction from high-dimensional protein biomarker data with missing values
key_results: Integrated random forest imputation, LASSO-penalized Cox regression, and random survival forests to identify stable prognostic biomarkers; demonstrated stability assessment and reduced overfitting risk in small-sample, high-dimensional settings
comments: The pipeline is transparent and reproducible but explicitly disclaims novelty in statistical methods—value lies in thoughtful workflow design for biomarker stability rather than algorithmic innovation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unsupervised imputation followed by LASSO-penalized Cox regression and random survival forest refinement reduces overfitting in small-sample biomarker studies
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated via publicly available proteomic cancer dataset

title: Design, synthesis, and in vivo antiepileptic evaluation of novel quinazolinone-phthalimide derivatives
task: Design and synthesis of novel antiepileptic quinazolinone-phthalimide compounds
key_results: Compounds 8j and 8k showed antiepileptic efficacy comparable to diazepam in pentylenetetrazole and lithium-pilocarpine seizure models with no mortality; docking and molecular dynamics confirmed GABAA receptor binding stability
comments: Traditional medicinal chemistry work with in vivo validation; molecular dynamics provides mechanistic support but clinical translation remains distant.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quinazolinone-phthalimide derivatives bind GABAA receptor benzodiazepine pocket and produce antiepileptic effects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; compounds 8j and 8k comparable to diazepam with favorable safety in animal seizure models

title: Mechanistic role of gut microbiota metabolites in hypertension-insomnia comorbidity via integrated network pharmacology and molecular dynamics
task: Network pharmacology analysis of gut microbiota metabolite involvement in hypertension-insomnia comorbidity
key_results: Identified 408 common targets between hypertension and insomnia; IL6 and PPARG as key hub proteins (12 connections each); IL-17 signaling pathway and lipid metabolism pinpointed; 3-indolepropionic acid and butyrate identified as promising metabolites with drug-like properties
comments: The integrative approach (target prediction, PPI, pathway enrichment, docking, MD simulations) is comprehensive but remains network-level mechanistic—empirical validation of metabolite supplementation effects is absent.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gut microbiota metabolites regulate IL-17 signaling to mediate hypertension-insomnia comorbidity
hypotheses.result: Supported by network analysis, pathway enrichment, and molecular docking; butyrate and 3-indolepropionic acid identified as key candidates with favorable pharmacokinetics

title: Variance-calibrated cross-individual bootstrapping for small-sample neuroscience
task: Confidence interval estimation for small-sample neuroscience data
key_results: CIB-VC achieved near-nominal 95% coverage across Gaussian, heavy-tailed, and lognormal distributions; produced ~23% narrower confidence intervals than hierarchical bootstrapping on weakly electric fish behavioral data (N=5)
comments: The method addresses a genuine reproducibility bottleneck in neuroscience, though validation on N=5 fish warrants cautious generalization to other small-sample contexts.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CIB-VC balances coverage and precision better than stratified or hierarchical bootstrapping under distributional skew
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via Monte Carlo simulations and empirical validation

title: Validation of conformal prediction in cervical atypia classification
task: Validation of conformal prediction for cervical cancer cell classification with uncertainty quantification
key_results: Expert annotation-based validation revealed conventional coverage metrics overestimate conformal prediction performance; current methods often produce prediction sets misaligned with human labels; identified capabilities for ambiguous and out-of-distribution detection
comments: The paper makes an important distinction between statistical coverage and practical utility—prediction sets may technically contain the true class while including implausible alternatives that confuse end users.

model_io.model: Conformal prediction framework applied to deep-learning classifiers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conformal prediction sets should align with human expert expectations, not merely achieve statistical coverage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; conventional coverage-based validation overestimates performance; sets often include false positives misaligned with human judgment

title: Characterization of T-cell receptor loci and expressed repertoire reveals a capacity for robust T-cell response in Atlantic cod (Gadus morhua)
task: Characterization of T-cell receptor loci and diversity in Atlantic cod
key_results: Comprehensive annotation revealed all four TCR chains on chromosomes 5 and 8; expressed repertoire showed surprisingly high diversity (TRA, TRB, TRD) with mostly private sequences and differential gene usage despite modest germline diversity
comments: The finding of robust expressed diversity despite loss of MHCII/CD4 genes challenges assumptions about immunocompetence requirements and provides a baseline for understanding alternative immune strategies in fish.

model_io.model: Genome annotation and TCR repertoire sequencing

hypotheses.hypothesis: Atlantic cod maintains diverse, mostly private TCR repertoires despite modest genomic diversity and absence of MHCII/CD4
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; high TRA, TRB, TRD diversity observed with untapped potential through differential gene usage

title: Screenathon 2.0: human–AI collaborative screening applied to patient-generated health data
task: Human-AI collaborative screening for systematic reviews
key_results: Screened 5,842 papers in 2 days across 11 disease topics with 27 expert partners; 487 relevant and 6,463 irrelevant records identified; post-screening survey showed increased user trust in AI-assisted reviewing
comments: While demonstrating scalability, the study emphasizes that crowdsourced human-AI collaboration requires substantial post-processing (reallocation, verification, noisy-label filtering) rather than being a plug-and-play approach.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Crowdsourced human-AI collaboration can accelerate systematic review screening while maintaining quality through post-processing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 5,842 papers screened in 2 days with thoughtful calibration and safeguards

title: Real-time decentralized model predictive control for cooperative multi-robot object transport: experimental validation
task: Decentralized MPC for multi-robot cooperative object transport
key_results: Real-time constrained optimization with accurate trajectory tracking; validated across point-to-point, curvilinear, and obstacle-rich scenarios with strict constraint satisfaction
comments: Solid experimental validation on physical testbed, but evaluation metrics (accuracy, constraint satisfaction) lack quantitative bounds; scalability to >2 robots unstated.

model_io.model: Decentralized Model Predictive Control with adaptive weighting

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decentralized MPC with joint-space coupling ensures global coordination while enabling local real-time optimization
hypotheses.result: Experiments show accurate trajectory tracking and constraint satisfaction; robustness to environmental uncertainties confirmed

title: A low-latency deep learning framework for volcanic ash cloud nowcasting using geostationary satellite imagery
task: Real-time volcanic ash nowcasting from satellite imagery
key_results: Structural similarity index of 0.88 for 15-minute forecasts; end-to-end latency <5 seconds on edge hardware (NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin)
comments: Strong practical deployment story with sub-5-second latency, but scenario injection framework is illustrative rather than validated against actual nuclear/volcanic data.

inputs: EUMETSAT SEVIRI Ash RGB composite imagery

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep learning can predict volcanic ash dispersion at near-real-time latency suitable for emergency response
hypotheses.result: Achieved 0.88 SSIM and <5s end-to-end latency on edge device

title: An advanced fermatean fuzzy DoC MCDM architecture for comprehensive quantitative assessment of physical fitness competency across academic institutions
task: Fermatean Fuzzy MCDM for institutional physical fitness assessment
key_results: Framework produces consistent fitness competency rankings from incomplete/uncertain expert data; integrates high-expressiveness uncertainty (FF sets) with transparent weight elicitation (DoC method)
comments: Methodologically sound application of advanced fuzzy logic, but case study is purely hypothetical with no institutional validation or comparative benchmarking against simpler methods.

inputs: expert assessments (endurance, strength, flexibility, body composition, mental well-being)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fermatean Fuzzy sets enable more expressive expert hesitation representation than standard/intuitionistic fuzzy sets
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated theoretically; practical advantage over FS/IFS/PFS not empirically quantified

title: A misclassification-aware explainable hybrid CNN-vision transformer framework for radiographic weld inspection
task: Explainable hybrid CNN-ViT for radiographic weld defect classification
key_results: 98.56% accuracy (vs 97.90% CNN baseline); 31% relative reduction in misclassification rate; validated on external GDXray dataset; fivefold cross-validation confirms generalization
comments: Strong empirical results with good explainability analysis (Grad-CAM, attention maps), but comparison limited to single lightweight CNN baseline; clinically relevant for manufacturing but not truly safety-critical validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid CNN-ViT with global contextual modeling reduces misclassification of similar defects vs CNN-only
hypotheses.result: 31% relative misclassification reduction; 98.56% accuracy achieved; cross-dataset generalization validated
